matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs2736100 (intronic TERT cancer- and telomere-associated SNP)

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed TERT variant places this sample in the curated TERT research context. The local TERT knowledge base spans telomere biology, cancer susceptibility, and promoter regulation. Common TERT variants are interpreted as research and risk-modifier signals, while the strongest clinical events often involve promoter mutations or rare high-impact pathogenic alleles. Common TERT polymorphisms are often interpreted through telomere-length, cancer-risk, or promoter-activity frameworks rather than as stand-alone diagnostic variants.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: TERT is central to telomere biology, cellular senescence, stem-cell maintenance, and oncogenesis.
Common germline SNPs and somatic promoter mutations are both heavily studied in cancer biology, but they represent different interpretive layers.
Cancer and telomerase-reactivation studies.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a TERT variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. TERT methylation is best interpreted as promoter and chromatin context around telomerase regulation. It can inform whether the locus appears permissive or repressed, but it is not a direct substitute for telomere-length or promoter-mutation testing. The local TERT knowledge base spans telomere biology, cancer susceptibility, and promoter regulation. Common TERT variants are interpreted as research and risk-modifier signals, while the strongest clinical events often involve promoter mutations or rare high-impact pathogenic alleles. Common TERT polymorphisms are often interpreted through telomere-length, cancer-risk, or promoter-activity frameworks rather than as stand-alone diagnostic variants.
